Changed By Date Version Notes: Christopher Veau 0 6 / 25 /2023 0.0 Original document. Tim Fischer 09/12/2023 0.0 Added Ctrl-G updates to Fill Transaction Christopher Veau 09/22/2023 0.0 Fill Pick Skip/Cancel handling EX83 – iLPN Status and Location Retention Base Pull iLPN List transac tion has some base options which lead to unlocated iLPNs . Unlocated iLPNs are iLPNs with a status of “In Inventory not Putaway” and no location . For McLane, h aving unlocated iLPNs leads to wave shorts as well as a disconnect between the physical and systematic information of the inventory. Additionally, with the Fill Pick Location transaction that is setup to prompt users for a quantity (Parameter 3-3 Item Verification mode set to “Item is displayed, user key qty” ) and users enter a quantity of 0 or a quantity less than expected quantity, there also result s in unlocated iLPNs . This modification puts away the iLPNs back to the location that they were in . Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Pull iLPN , RF Pick Location Reporting N Other N For certain base options on the Pull iLPN List transaction b ase WM removes the allocations from the LPN and also removes the iLPN from the location i t was in. These options are the following Skip/Cancel (Ctrl-G) Skip/Cancel/Lock (Ctrl-S) Alternate/Lock (Ctrl-B) Sub/Lock (Ctrl-Z) Substitute (CTRL +T) With this modification WMOS is enhanced in such a way that the original iLPN which was tied to the task detail is putaway back to the location it was previously in since base has taken that iLPN out of inventory. When the Fill Pick location transaction has Parameter 3-3 set to “Item is displayed, user key qty” the user is prompted for the quantity to fill the location. Whenever the user enters a quantity less than the prompted quantity, base WM adjusts the iLPN quantity and then the iLPN removed from the location. Additionally for the base option Skip/Cancel (Ctrl-G) on the Fill Pick Location transaction WM removes the allocations from the LPN and also removes the iLPN from the location it was in. With this modification WMOS is enhanced in such a way that the iLPN is putaway back to the location it was previously in since base has taken that iLPN out of inventory . Assumptions Fill Active Transaction has Parameter 3-3 Item Verification mode set to “Item is displayed, user key qty” Fill Case Pick Transaction has Parameter 3-3 Item Verification mode set to “Item is displayed, user key qty” This modification only affects Pull iLPN List transactions and the Fill Pick Transactions For Pull iLPN transactions it is assumed that users are completing the task after having used an option mentioned above. This modification applies to INT1, INT3, and INT9 tasks This modification does not make any changes to base functionality. This includes but is not limited to , the application of lock codes and creation of cycle counts if the system is configured as such. The same User Directed Putaway transaction leveraged in EX54 for putting away excess inventory is also used in this modification for the Fill Pick transac t ion s For RF Fill Pick Location transactions, if EX54 trans param 504-5 04 is enabled, then EX83 functionality is disabled due to EX54 modification handling the appropriate updates Dependencies Code Dependencies Interface Dependencies N/A P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 RF Pull iLPN List Transaction Enablement IF C-00 0 -000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement) IF C-000- EX83 byte 1 = Y (EX 8 3 extension enablement) ELSE the EX83 functionality is disabled ELSE the EX83 functionality is disabled ELSE the EX83 functionality disabled Additionally , the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF Pull iLPN List transaction parameter byte 50 3 -5 03 . This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 83 is turned off, and no custom EX 83 processing will occur. Validations No custom validations needed for this mod ification. Validate that system code C-E83-001 has a valid User Directed Putaway transaction link name . If not exit custom code. Updates Whenever a user uses the below options on the pull iLPN list transaction the or iginal iLPN that was associated with the task detail gets updates by base and end up in an “In Inventory not Putaway” status . Skip/Cancel (Ctrl-G) Skip/Cancel/Lock (Ctrl-S) Alternate/Lock (Ctrl-B) Sub/Lock (Ctrl-Z) Once this happens, the original iLPN is systematically putaway to the LPN.PREV_SUB_LOCN using the base user directed transaction specified with transaction found in C-E83-001 system code. . The base user directed putaway will update the iLPNs status to “Putaway” and the iLPN will now be back in the previous location it was in. NOTE: If the facility warehouse parameter “Lost in Warehouse” under the Default iLPN Locks section is set to a value then , via base user directed putaway , that lock will be removed and the iLPN will be put away to the location. Navigation No changes to base navigation as part of this extension Outputs No changes to outputs as part of this extension. Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base LM Hooks as part of this extension . RF Fill Pick Location Transactions Enablement IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement) IF C-000- EX83 byte 1 = Y (EX 80 extension enablement) ELSE the EX83 functionality is disabled ELSE the EX83 functionality is disabled ELSE the EX83 functionality disabled Additionally, the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF Fill Pick Location transaction parameter byte 503-503. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX83 is turned off, and no custom EX83 processing will occur. Validations For updates happening on the quantity adjustments If EX54 functionality is turned on for the RF Fill Pick Location transaction then the EX83 functionality is disabled since the EX54 modification will take care of the appropriate updates. That is IF RF Fill Pick Location transaction parameter byte 504-504 is set to ‘ N ’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘ Y ’, the extension specific control for EX 54 is turned ON , then no custom EX83 processing will occur. IF Skip/Cancel func tionality is envoked Then regardless if the EX54 functionality is on or off the RF Fill Pick Location transaction then the EX83 functionality is ENABLED. That is IF RF Fill Pick Location transaction parameter byte 504-504 is set to ‘N’ or ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Additionally validate that system code C-E83-001 has a valid User Directed Putaway transaction link name. If not exit custom code. Updates Whenever a user enters a quantity less than the prompted quantity, base WM splits the filled quantity off the original LPN and then the original LPN is removed from the location and gets updated to an “In Inventory not Putaway” status . Or , whenever a user uses the below options on the Fill Pick Location transaction the original iLPN that was associated with the task detail gets updates by base and ends up in an “In Inventory not Putaway” status. Skip/Cancel (Ctrl-G) Once this happens, t he original iLPN is systematically putaway to the LPN.PREV_SUB_LOCN using the user directed transaction specified with transaction found in C-E83 -001 system code. . The base user directed putaway will update the iLPNs status to “Putaway” and the iLPN will now be back in the previous location it was in. Navigation No changes to base navigation as part of this extension Outputs No changes to outputs as part of this extension. Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base LM Hooks as part of this extension. Process Change/Addition No additional process changes in scope for this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Custom Configurations System Code Types Rec Type Code Type Code Desc RF Desc Max Len .
